flow_points: Australia started strong, scoring 64 runs for 2 wickets in the Powerplay (RR 10.67).
JP Inglis anchored Australia's batting, contributing 20.3% of the team's runs.
In response, Ireland struggled in their Powerplay, scoring only 40 runs for 4 wickets (RR 6.67).
Ireland's chase was severely hampered by losing 4 early wickets in the Powerplay.
NT Ellis delivered a crucial spell, taking 4 wickets for 13 runs in 3.5 overs.
A Zampa also delivered a crucial spell, securing 4 wickets for 25 runs in 4 overs.
Ellis sustained defensive pressure throughout, finishing with an impact score of 150.0.
Ireland struggled under extreme chase pressure (Index: 95.0), leading to a late-innings collapse.
Australia ultimately won the match by 67 runs.
